subtitle: The Poet-Emperor and the Uprising of Delhi

panels.shot: majestic low-angle wide of the Red Fort ramparts at dusk, with Bahadur Shah Zafar silhouetted in an archway
panels.action: Bahadur Shah Zafar stands frail yet upright in a Red Fort balcony arch, his white beard catching the last orange light as the massive red sandstone walls of Shahjahanabad stretch below him into the smoky horizon.

dialogue.text: Delhi, 1857: as the British Raj trembled, an ageing poet in the Red Fort became the last Mughal emperor and a reluctant banner for a city in revolt.

i18n.hi: दिल्ली, 1857: जब ब्रिटिश राज डगमगा रहा था, लाल क़िले में बैठा एक बूढ़ा शायर आख़िरी बादशाह बना और बग़ावती शहर का अनचाहा झंडा।

panels.shot: slow push-in from bird's-eye over Shahjahanabad towards the Red Fort in the early morning haze
panels.action: From high above, the walled city of Shahjahanabad sprawls along the Yamuna, its narrow lanes and domes shrouded in dawn mist, while the Red Fort rises like a red ship on the river’s edge, smoke from cooking fires curling upwards.

dialogue.text: Shahjahanabad, the city of emperors, had long fallen into British shadow. Yet within its red walls, an old throne and an older dream still survived in whispers and verse.

i18n.hi: शाहजहानाबाद, बादशाहों का शहर, बरसों से अंग्रेज़ी साए में था। पर इन लाल दीवारों के अंदर, एक पुराना तख़्त और उससे पुराना ख़्वाब फुसफ़ुसाहटों और शेरों में ज़िंदा था।

panels.shot: intimate interior mid-shot in the zenana courtyard, focusing on Zafar seated among manuscripts and pens
panels.action: Bahadur Shah Zafar sits cross-legged on a simple carpet in a shaded courtyard of the Red Fort, ink-stained fingers resting on open Persian and Urdu manuscripts, while a veiled attendant quietly fans away the heavy Delhi heat.

dialogue.text: Bahadur Shah Zafar, more poet than prince, ruled little beyond these rooms. The Company collected the revenue; the emperor collected couplets and the tired loyalties of an old court.

i18n.hi: बहादुर शाह ज़फ़र, बादशाह से ज़्यादा शायर, इन कमरों से आगे कुछ न चला सके। कंपनी खज़ाना लेती, बादशाह बस ग़ज़लें और बूढ़ी वफ़ादारियाँ।

panels.shot: close-up on Zafar’s lined face as distant cannon echoes reach him
panels.action: Zafar’s eyes lift sharply from a half-written ghazal as the faint boom of cannon-fire rolls across the marble hall, his brows knitting in concern while a small porcelain inkpot trembles on the low desk.

dialogue.text: In May 1857, the thunder came from beyond Delhi. Sepoys, enraged by greased cartridges and years of humiliation, marched from Meerut toward the old imperial city.

i18n.hi: मई 1857 में गरज शहर के बाहर से उठी। कारतूसों की बेइज़्ज़ती और बरसों की ज़िल्लत से तंग सिपाही मेरठ से निकल पुरानी शाही दिल्ली की ओर चले।

panels.shot: wide shot of Lahori Gate courtyard as exhausted sepoys pour in, with Zafar in the background arch
panels.action: Dust-covered sepoys, turbans askew and faces streaked with fatigue, surge into the Lahori Gate courtyard of the Red Fort, muskets slung, while in a distant archway Bahadur Shah Zafar stands, slight and still, watching their arrival with uneasy attention.

dialogue.text: They burst into Delhi, slew British officers, and poured into the Red Fort, crying for the emperor. The Company’s rule, so solid on paper, shook under the weight of desperate men.

i18n.hi: वे दिल्ली में घुसे, अंग्रेज़ अफ़सरों को मारा, और लाल क़िले में उमड़ पड़े, बादशाह को पुकारते हुए। काग़ज़ पर मज़बूत कंपनी का राज हताश सिपाहियों के बोझ तले कांप उठा।

panels.shot: medium low-angle inside Diwan-i-Khas, with sepoy representatives imploring Zafar seated on a modest throne
panels.action: In the Diwan-i-Khas, beneath faded gilded arches, Zafar sits on a modest cushioned seat, while a handful of sepoy leaders stand before him, hands raised in urgent appeal, faces flushed with heat and hope.

dialogue.text: The sepoys demanded what history had long denied him: to be emperor in deed, not only in name. Their revolt needed a seal; their swords needed a signature.

i18n.hi: सिपाहियों ने वह माँगा जो तारीख़ ने उससे छीन रखा था: नाम के नहीं, सच के बादशाह बनो। उनकी बग़ावत को मुहर चाहिए थी, उनकी तलवारों को दस्तख़त।

panels.shot: tight three-quarter close-up on Zafar’s thoughtful face, shadows of jali lattice on his features
panels.action: Soft lattice shadows fall across Zafar’s lined face as he gazes downward in troubled thought, fingers lightly gripping a carved armrest, the faint glint of the Peacock Throne’s lost place in his eyes.

dialogue.text: He was seventy-two, a pensioner of the Company, cautious and weary. To accept was to invite ruin; to refuse was to abandon a city already in the storm’s path.

i18n.hi: वह बहत्तर का, कंपनी का मुंशी, थका और सतर्क। मानना तबाही बुलाना था, मना करना तूफ़ान में घिरे शहर को अकेला छोड़ना।

panels.shot: dramatic side-profile mid-shot as Zafar raises a frail hand to address assembled courtiers and sepoys
panels.action: Zafar sits upright, profile sharp against a dim marble backdrop, as he slowly raises his right hand, frail veins visible, while courtiers and sepoys stand tense and silent in the crowded Diwan-i-Khas.

dialogue.speaker: bahadur_shah_zafar
dialogue.text: If the people of Hindustan look to this throne, I cannot turn away.

i18n.hi: अगर हिंदुस्तान की नज़र इस तख़्त पर है, तो मैं मुँह नहीं मोड़ सकता।

panels.shot: wide interior of Diwan-i-‘Am with a mixed crowd of Hindu and Muslim notables listening to Zafar seated under the canopy
panels.action: In the Diwan-i-‘Am, Bahadur Shah Zafar sits beneath a worn canopy, surrounded by qadis, Hindu merchants, and city elders, their different turbans and robes forming a patchwork of Delhi’s communities as they lean in to hear him.

dialogue.text: Letters went out in his name, calling princes, landlords, and towns to unite. In the fragile script of a poet, Hindu and Muslim, Rajput and Rohilla, were urged to stand as one against the Company.

i18n.hi: उसके नाम से खत निकले, राजाओं, ज़मींदारों, क़स्बों को पुकारते हुए। नाज़ुक शायराना लफ़्ज़ों में, हिंदू-मुसलमान, राजपूत-रोहिल्ला सबको कंपनी के ख़िलाफ़ एकजुट होने को कहा गया।

panels.shot: montage-like wide shot from above of rebel councils in the Red Fort, with Zafar slightly apart
panels.action: Viewed from a high balcony, rebel officers argue around low tables piled with maps and petitions, while at one side Bahadur Shah Zafar sits slightly withdrawn, listening, a small oil lamp casting a halo around him.

dialogue.text: Yet power in Delhi lay in many hands. Sepoy leaders, princes like Mirza Mughal, city chiefs and clerics all pulled at the reins, while the emperor’s seal lent their quarrels the weight of the Mughal name.

i18n.hi: पर दिल्ली की ताक़त कई हाथों में बँटी थी। सिपाही सरदार, मिर्ज़ा मुग़ल जैसे शहज़ादे, मुहल्ला सरदार और मौलवी सब रस्सी खींचते रहे, और बादशाह की मुहर से इन झगड़ों को भी मुग़ल वज़न मिल गया।

panels.shot: evening mid-shot of Zafar mediating between two heated rebel leaders in a lamplit chamber
panels.action: By the light of smoking oil lamps, Zafar sits between two angry rebel captains, each leaning forward in heated gesture, while he raises both palms in a calming plea, his expression tired yet firm.

dialogue.text: He tried to restrain excess, to keep order, to shield Christians and loyalists where he could. But his words traveled slowly, and muskets answered faster than decrees.

i18n.hi: वह ज़्यादतियाँ रोकने, क़ायदा रखने, और जहाँ हो सके ईसाइयों व वफ़ादारों को बचाने की कोशिश करता रहा। पर उसके फ़रमान धीमे चलते, बंदूकों के जवाब तेज़ आते।

panels.shot: wide view from the fort walls of British batteries across the Yamuna, with Zafar in foreground
panels.action: Standing on the high red ramparts, Bahadur Shah Zafar grips the parapet, his small figure framed against the sky, as across the Yamuna British guns line the Ridge, their dark mouths trained on the city.

dialogue.text: Through the monsoon and heat, the British assembled on the Ridge. Siege guns spoke daily, shells arching over the river, while famine, suspicion, and disease crept through Delhi’s alleys.

i18n.hi: बरसात और लू में, अंग्रेज़ रिज पर जुटे। घेराबंदी की तोपें रोज़ गरजीं, गोलों ने दरिया के ऊपर से रास्ता बनाया, और भूख, बेएतबारी, बीमारी चुपके से दिल्ली की गलियों में उतरी।

panels.shot: interior mid-shot of Zafar receiving alarming news from a breathless messenger, papers scattered
panels.action: A breathless messenger, clothes stained with dust, bows low before Zafar in a darkened hall, his hand outstretched with a crumpled note, while scattered petitions and letters lie untidy at the emperor’s feet.

dialogue.text: As weeks dragged on, rebel discipline frayed. Some sought plunder; others sought compromise. For every order Zafar signed, another was ignored, questioned, or overtaken by the chaos of war.

i18n.hi: जैसे-जैसे हफ़्ते खिंचे, बग़ावत का अनुशासन ढीला पड़ा। कुछ लूट में लगे, कुछ सुलह ढूँढने लगे। ज़फ़र की हर हुक्मनामा पर एक और हुक्म अनसुना, सवालों में, या जंग की अफ़रातफ़री में दब गया।

panels.shot: dramatic wide shot of British assault on the Kashmiri Gate, seen faintly from the fort battlements where Zafar stands
panels.action: From the distant Red Fort wall, Zafar’s slight figure watches a haze of smoke and dust billow around the Kashmiri Gate, tiny silhouettes of British and rebel soldiers locked in a furious but indistinct struggle amid crumbling masonry.

dialogue.text: In September, the breach came at Kashmiri Gate. Street by street, house by house, British troops fought their way into Shahjahanabad, the city’s cries drowned by cannon and command.

i18n.hi: सितंबर में कश्मीरी गेट पर सेंध लग गई। गली दर गली, मकान दर मकान, अंग्रेज़ सिपाही शाहजहानाबाद में लड़े, तोप और हुक्म की गूँज में शहर की चीखें डूब गईं।

panels.shot: somber interior mid-shot of Zafar in a near-deserted Red Fort hall, pillars casting long shadows
panels.action: The once-crowded hall of the Red Fort stands nearly empty, pillars casting long shadows, as Bahadur Shah Zafar sits alone on a low cushion, shoulders bent, the distant rumble of fighting echoing through the stone.

dialogue.text: Rebel officers slipped away; allies vanished into the countryside. The emperor who had been lifted onto a crumbling throne now faced the fall almost alone.

i18n.hi: बाग़ी अफ़सर फिसलकर भाग निकले; साथी देहात में ओझल हो गए। जिस बादशाह को टूटी तख़्त पर चढ़ाया गया था, अब वही अकेला पतन का सामना कर रहा था।

panels.shot: twilight wide shot at Humayun’s Tomb, with Zafar seated under the great dome among a small retinue
panels.action: Under the vast white dome of Humayun’s Tomb, Bahadur Shah Zafar sits on a simple mat in the central chamber, a few loyal attendants and women of the household gathered anxiously around, as fading light filters through the high arches.

dialogue.text: He left the fort for Humayun’s Tomb, seeking refuge among the dead emperors. The Mughal line, which had risen with Babur’s march, now huddled in the shadow of a tomb.

i18n.hi: वह क़िला छोड़ हमायूँ के मक़बरे पहुँचा, मरे बादशाहों के बीच पनाह ढूँढने। बाबर की चढ़ाई से उठी मुग़ल नस्ल अब एक क़ब्र की छाँव में सिमट गई।

panels.shot: somber mid-shot of Zafar confronted by a British officer and soldiers in the tomb’s arcade
panels.action: In the cool arcade of the tomb, Bahadur Shah Zafar stands facing a British officer in uniform, backed by armed soldiers, while members of his household watch from behind pillars, their faces tense and unreadable.

dialogue.text: Soon the British found him there. Surrounded by redcoats and sepoys in Company service, the last Mughal surrendered not with a sword drawn, but with a quiet, resigned gaze.

i18n.hi: जल्द ही अंग्रेज़ों ने उसे वहीं पा लिया। लाल कोटियों और कंपनी के सिपाहियों से घिरा आख़िरी मुग़ल न तलवार खींचकर, बल्कि चुप, थकी नज़रों से सरेंडर हुआ।

panels.shot: interior courtroom-style wide shot of the Red Fort trial, with Zafar seated as a prisoner before British officers
panels.action: In a stripped hall of the Red Fort, Bahadur Shah Zafar sits on a plain wooden bench, hands folded in his lap, while British officers in stiff uniforms sit at a table before him, papers and inkstands neatly arranged.

dialogue.text: Tried by a European Military Commission, he faced charges of rebellion, treason, and murder. In that same fort where he had once been crowned, he now answered as a prisoner to foreign judges.

i18n.hi: यूरोपीय मिलिटरी कमीशन ने उसे घेरा; इलज़ाम थे ग़द्दारी, बग़ावत, क़त्ल के। उसी क़िले में जहाँ कभी ताज पहनाया गया, अब वह कैदी बनकर पराए क़ाज़ियों के सामने जवाब दे रहा था।

panels.shot: close-up on Zafar during the trial, eyes distant, as he responds calmly
panels.action: Zafar’s face fills the frame, expression calm but distant, as he speaks softly, a faint sheen of sweat on his brow, while blurred British uniforms occupy the background in cold, rigid lines.

dialogue.speaker: bahadur_shah_zafar
dialogue.text: I was a helpless old man. My name was used by those with the sword.

i18n.hi: मैं तो लाचार बूढ़ा था। मेरा नाम तलवार वालों ने बरता।

panels.shot: wide shot of Zafar in a bullock cart leaving Delhi, the city walls receding behind
panels.action: A simple covered bullock cart rolls slowly away from the skyline of Delhi, the Red Fort and Jama Masjid receding in the hazy distance, as Bahadur Shah Zafar sits inside, profile visible through the curtain gap, gaze fixed on the fading city.

dialogue.text: Found guilty, he was exiled to Rangoon. Without ceremony, the last emperor of Hindustan left his capital in a bullock cart, the red walls shrinking behind him like a vanished mirage.

i18n.hi: कसूरवार क़रार देकर उसे रंगून भेज दिया गया। बिना तामझाम के, हिंदुस्तान का आख़िरी बादशाह बैलगाड़ी में राजधानी से निकला, लाल दीवारें उसके पीछे मृगतृष्णा-सी सिमटती गईं।

panels.shot: intimate close-up in Rangoon, Zafar writing poetry in a dim cell-like room
panels.action: In a dim, bare room with peeling plaster, Bahadur Shah Zafar sits by a small window, hunched over a scrap of paper, lips moving silently as he writes, the faint outline of palm trees and distant water beyond.

dialogue.text: In Rangoon, stripped of court and crown, he returned to the only realm left to him: verse. In his laments, Delhi lived on as a lost beloved and a broken trust.

i18n.hi: रंगून में, दरबार और ताज से खाली, वह अपने एकमात्र बची बादशाहत में लौटा: शायरी। उसकी मसनवियों में दिल्ली एक खोई महबूबा और टूटा हुआ अमानत बनकर जीती रही।

panels.shot: final reflective wide shot of an empty Diwan-i-Khas today, sunlit and silent, with a faint ghostly overlay of Zafar’s seated figure
panels.action: The Diwan-i-Khas stands empty in bright daylight, polished marble floor gleaming, parrots wheeling above the silent arches, while a faint, translucent image of Bahadur Shah Zafar seated cross-legged appears like memory at the center.

dialogue.text: His command over armies was brittle, his authority contested, yet his name bound a divided city for a brief, fierce season. In the story of 1857, Bahadur Shah Zafar endures as a fragile, human banner of a people’s longing for their own rule.

i18n.hi: उसका फ़ौजों पर हुक्म कमज़ोर, उसका असर हरदम चुनौती में, पर उसका नाम कुछ समय को बंटी दिल्ली को एक धागे में बाँध गया। 1857 की कहानी में बहादुर शाह ज़फ़र जनता की स्वराज की तड़प का नाज़ुक, इंसानी परचम बनकर ज़िंदा रहता है।

title_i18n.en: Bahadur Shah Zafar and the Red Fort Call
title_i18n.hi: बहादुर शाह ज़फ़र और लाल क़िले की पुकार

subtitle_i18n.en: The Poet-Emperor and the Uprising of Delhi
